agentMandates: Identify explicit requirement violations using the common schema.
configuration.notes: Live smoke test only; not part of the primary comparison.

findings.title: Authorization check missing for admin-only operation
findings.description: The patch attempts to create an invite but only checks for membership existence. It does not verify that the user is a team administrator. This allows non-admin members to create invitations, violating REQ-AUTH-1 which requires only team administrators may create invitations.

reviewers.title: Unauthorized team invitation creation
reviewers.description: The patch creates a new invites route but authorizes by checking membership only. It does not verify that the requester is a team administrator as required by REQ-AUTH-1. Any member (non-admin) could pass the 403 check if membership exists, or potential gaps in admin verification could allow invitation creation.

reviewers.title: Authorization check missing for invite creation
reviewers.description: The patch adds a new route that creates invites but relies on a membership check that may not verify the requester is a team administrator. It checks for membership existence but does not ensure the user has an administrator role or permissions required to create invitations, which violates REQ-AUTH-1: Only team administrators may create invitations.

reviewers.title: Authorization check missing for invitation creation
reviewers.description: The patch creates a new invites route but only checks membership for the requesting user. It does not verify that the user is a team administrator. Missing explicit check against admin role violates REQ-AUTH-1, which requires only team administrators to create invitations.

principal.title: Authorization check for invite creation missing admin verification
principal.description: The patch creates a new invites route that authorizes by membership existence only and does not verify that the requester has an administrator role required to create invitations, violating REQ-AUTH-1: Only team administrators may create invitations.

configuration.notes: Live smoke test only; not part of the primary comparison.

reviewers.title: Authorization rule violation: invites creation not restricted to administrators
reviewers.description: The new route for creating invites allows any user with a membership in the team to create an invite. It checks for membership but does not verify that the member is a team administrator as required by REQ-AUTH-1.

reviewers.title: Unauthorized team invites flow exists but relies on missing route guard context
reviewers.description: The new route for creating invites checks membership by fetching membership and returning 403 if not found, but this is implemented in a way that depends on an undefined context: user and teamId are used without shown extraction from the request, and the code snippet shows a direct await on findMembership and request.json() without clearly defined authentication/authorization middleware. If the route does not ensure that only team administrators can create invites, this could allow non-admin members to trigger invites. The explicit product requirement REQ-AUTH-1 states only team administrators may create invitations; the patch does not demonstrate enforcement for administrator role distinct from general membership.

reviewers.title: Authorization check for creating invitations missing explicit enforcement of REQ-AUTH-1
reviewers.description: The patch adds a route for creating invites but only shows a single authorization check by finding membership and returning 403 if not found. It does not explicitly enforce that only team administrators can create invitations; membership alone may not guarantee admin rights. There is no explicit role/permission verification against an administrator role before allowing createInvite.

reviewers.title: Authorization rule violation: invites creation not restricted to administrators
reviewers.description: The new route for creating invites allows any user with a membership in the team to create an invite. It checks for membership but does not verify that the member is a team administrator as required by REQ-AUTH-1.
